global_rules.claim_hygiene_policy: The compressed P trunk remains candidate/audit metadata until the Ward-projected Thomson endpoint, RG/matching/scheme package, interval certificate, and live-code adoption gate all close. Do not use measured alpha(0) as a solver input.
global_scope_guard: Address remaining blockers as coupled closure bundles, not one-by-one issue patches.
Keep theorem-grade, selected-class, continuation, and compare-only rows mechanically distinct.
If a bundle cannot close, return the minimal obstruction and the exact surfaces that must remain claim-safe.

tasks.summary: Close or sharply obstruct the remaining particle derivation gaps through coupled bundle packets.
tasks.title: Particle Gap Bundle Closure

acceptance_criteria: Treat issue 223, issue 32, and issue 224 as one electroweak root package.
Either define a source-emitted Ward-projected Thomson endpoint object with matching and interval-certificate requirements, or return the minimal obstruction list.
Do not import measured alpha(0), a hidden Thomson endpoint, or a compare-only charged bundle as a solver input.
tasks.prompt_focus: Close or sharply obstruct the coupled electroweak root package: compressed P trunk, Ward-projected Thomson endpoint, RG/matching, interval certificate, and live adoption gate.
tasks.summary: Worker packet for the P-root and low-energy electromagnetic endpoint closure bundle.
tasks.title: Electroweak root closure bundle

acceptance_criteria: Treat charged, quark, and neutrino source gaps as a shared excitation/trace-lift problem.
Either propose one reusable source theorem object or return the minimal separate obstructions.
Keep exact theorem rows, selected-class rows, and comparison-tension rows separated.
tasks.prompt_focus: Search for a shared source theorem across charged leptons, quarks, and neutrinos, or return the minimal lane-specific obstructions.
tasks.summary: Worker packet for shared spectrum-source closure across charged, quark, and neutrino lanes.
tasks.title: Spectrum source closure bundle

acceptance_criteria: Treat hadron production systematics and the hadronic Thomson spectral object as one coupled backend problem.
Either define the production artifact schema for rho_had(s;P) and its uncertainty budget, or return the smallest missing QCD primitive.
Do not promote surrogate hadron artifacts without continuum, volume, chiral, and statistical budgets.
tasks.prompt_focus: Connect the hadron production backend to the hadronic spectral object required by the Ward-projected Thomson endpoint.
tasks.summary: Worker packet for QCD production and Thomson hadronic spectral-object closure.
tasks.title: QCD Thomson backend closure bundle

acceptance_criteria: Integrate all returned bundle packets before deciding whether live prediction roots may change.
Promote the compressed P trunk only if endpoint, matching, interval, hadronic, and source-object requirements are all closed.
If any requirement remains open, keep the trunk as candidate/audit metadata and update the ledger with the next bundle revision.
tasks.prompt_focus: Integrate the three bundle packets and decide whether the compressed P trunk may become the live particle root, defaulting to keep-candidate unless all gates close.
tasks.summary: Integration gate for the bundled closure campaign.
tasks.title: Particle root integration gate
